title: 10 best herbal creams for hypertension

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to help lower blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that constrict blood vessels, reducing peripheral resistance and improving circulation.
Licoricidin: This compound has been found to have a vasodilatory effect, which means it helps relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, both of which can help reduce blood pressure.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice cream contains flavonoids like glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect against the damage caused by high blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to lower cortisol levels in the body, which in turn helps to regulate blood pressure. The flavonoids and saponins present in Glycyrrhiza glabra have been shown to have a vasodilatory effect, causing blood vessels to relax and widen, thereby reducing blood pressure. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce stress on the cardiovascular system, contributing to its overall blood pressure-lowering effects.
remedy_constituents: Licorinic acid: It helps with hypertension by inhibiting the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 2 (11β-HSD2), which is involved in the regulation of blood pressure by preventing the conversion of cortisol to its active form.

Glycyrrhizin: It helps with hypertension by reducing aldosterone levels, which in turn reduces sodium retention in the kidneys and decreases blood pressure. It also has a direct vasodilatory effect, causing blood vessels to relax and widen.

Flavonoids (specifically isoflavonoids): They help with hypertension by acting as endothelium-dependent vasodilators, promoting the release of nitric oxide and thereby relaxing blood vessels, reducing peripheral resistance, and lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder.
Steep 250 grams of the powder in 2 liters of water for 30 minutes to create an infusion.
Combine 1 liter of the infusion with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Strain the mixture and whip it with 50 grams of shea butter until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Relaxation and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to high blood pressure.
Vasodilation: Valerian's ability to dilate blood vessels helps to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure by allowing blood to flow more easily through the veins and arteries.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high blood pressure and cardiovascular disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to promote relaxation and calmness in the body. The active compounds in valerian, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to high blood pressure. Additionally, valerian creams may also help to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted in individuals with hypertension, further reducing blood pressure levels.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This terpene helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can contribute to lowering blood pressure by reducing stress-induced hypertension.
Valeranone: This sesquiterpene acts as a sedative and anxiolytic, helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation, both of which can help to lower blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.
Valerianol: This terpene has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure and alleviating hypertension symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root is used due to its high content of valerenic acid, valeranone, and valepotriates, which have sedative and anti-hypertensive properties.
Seeds: Valerian seeds are used for their potential to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health due to the presence of valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated for 2-3 hours.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 100 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of valeriana officinalis powder with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10-15 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce blood pressure by decreasing inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Calming and sedative effects: The soothing and calming properties of chamomile help relax the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety, both of which are common triggers for hypertension.
Vasodilation and relaxation: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids cause blood vessels to dilate, or widen, which can lower blood pressure by allowing blood to flow more freely through the vessels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with hypertension because of its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress, two major contributors to high blood pressure. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile help to dilate blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can help individuals manage anxiety, a common comorbidity with hypertension. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Matricaria chamomilla creams may offer a natural and effective approach to managing hypertension.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a calcium channel blocker, which helps in relaxing blood vessels and reducing blood pressure.

Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, ultimately contributing to blood pressure control.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has a vasodilatory effect, which helps in widening blood vessels and improving blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high apigenin and luteolin content, which have been shown to have a potential role in lowering blood pressure.
Seeds: They are used for their alpha-bisabolol content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate hypertension symptoms.
Leaves: They are used due to their flavonoid and terpenoid content, which have been shown to have a potential role in lowering blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a clean and well-drained area after 2-3 weeks of drying.
Infuse 50g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the infused matricaria chamomilla mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler at a temperature of 180-190°f for 10 minutes.
Combine the infused matricaria chamomilla liquid with the beeswax mixture and stir until a smooth consistency is achieved after 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Hypericin: This active compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce blood pressure by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels.
Hypotensive flavonoids: Flavonoids present in St. John's Wort have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help lower blood pressure.

Adaptogenic terpenes: Terpenes such as limonene and sesquiterpene lactones in St. John's Wort have an adaptogenic effect, helping the body adapt to stress and reducing anxiety, a common comorbidity with hypertension.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, creams helps with hypertension because they contain hypericin and hyperforin, bioactive compounds that have vasodilatory properties. These compounds help to relax and dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of St. John's Wort creams may also contribute to their blood pressure-lowering effects by reducing inflammation in the blood vessels and improving endothelial function, ultimately helping to regulate blood pressure and promote cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
Quercetin: It acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to prevent the damage caused by high blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
Kaempferol: It has a potential diuretic effect, which helps to increase urine production and reduce fluid buildup in the body, ultimately lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their potential blood pressure-lowering effects.
Flowers: They are a rich source of naphthoquinones, such as hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have vasodilatory properties and help lower blood pressure.
Roots: They contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, contributing to their potential use in treating hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves by cutting them just above the node.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm air circulation oven at 30-40°c for 2-3 hours.
Mix 20-30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 milliliters of coconut oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 30-40 minutes then stir until it reaches 45-50°c.
Pour the mixture into jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 2-3 hours.

remedy_properties: Potassium content: Dandelion cream contains high levels of potassium, which can help counterbalance the effects of sodium and reduce blood pressure by promoting healthy blood vessel function.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in dandelion cream have been shown to improve blood flow, relax blood vessels, and decrease inflammation, all of which can contribute to a reduction in blood pressure.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as taraxasterol, found in dandelion cream, have been demonstrated to exhibit anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which can help reduce blood pressure by improving blood vessel elasticity and reducing resistance.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with hypertension because of its diuretic properties that aid in the removal of excess fluids from the body. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and inulin, have been shown to help regulate blood pressure by promoting vasodilation, which is the widening of blood vessels. This increase in blood vessel diameter reduces resistance, allowing blood to flow more easily and lowering blood pressure. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its blood pressure-lowering effects.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin helps with hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which plays a key role in blood pressure regulation.

Phenolic acids (including Chlorogenic acid): These compounds help with hypertension by scavenging free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and improving blood vessel function, leading to lower blood pressure.

Inulin: This polysaccharide helps with hypertension by reducing blood pressure through its diuretic effects, which increase urine production and help remove excess fluids from the body, leading to lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to have anti-hypertensive properties.
Roots: They are rich in inulin and saponins, which can help lower blood pressure by reducing the heart rate and blood vessel constriction.
Buds: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may contribute to reduced blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for 2 hours.
Chop 20 grams of taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife for 10 minutes.
Infuse 20 grams of chopped taraxacum officinale in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed oil for 4 hours under the sun.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids for 30 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 50 milliliters of the strained oil and heat the mixture for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Potassium content: Herbal aloe creams contain high amounts of potassium, which helps to counterbalance the effects of sodium and reduce blood pressure by promoting healthy blood vessel function.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to hypertension, and also improve overall cardiovascular health.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with hypertension because it contains compounds that help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera reduce inflammation in blood vessels, making them more flexible and less prone to constriction. Additionally, aloe vera's potassium content helps to counterbalance the effects of sodium and promote healthy blood vessel function. This leads to improved circulation and reduced blood pressure, making it a potential natural remedy for hypertension management.
remedy_constituents: Bradykinin: It helps with hypertension by promoting vasodilation and reducing blood pressure through its action on the endothelium.

Anthraquinones (Aloin, Aloe-emodin, and Aloe-pectin): These compounds help with hypertension by inhibiting the Na+/K+-ATPase enzyme, reducing sodium reabsorption in the kidneys, and subsequently lowering blood pressure.

Phenolic Acids (Caffeic acid, Ferulic acid, and Gallic acid): These compounds help with hypertension by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing oxidative stress, and preventing endothelial dysfunction.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have a potential vasodilatory effect that can help lower blood pressure.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera contain saponins, which have been found to have a hypotensive effect by reducing blood pressure and heart rate.
Roots: The roots of Aloe vera are a good source of anthraquinones, which have been used to treat hypertension by reducing blood pressure and alleviating symptoms of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 aloe vera leaves from mature plants with thick fleshy leaves.
Gently peel the outer skin of aloe vera leaves with a sharp knife.
Grind 100g of peeled aloe vera pulp into a fine paste using a blender.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 50g of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine 100g of aloe vera paste with the cooled wax-oil mixture and stir until smooth.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Ginkgo biloba extracts contain flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood vessel function and reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving vascular reactivity.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hypertension.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids present in Maidenhair Tree extracts have been shown to have a hypotensive effect by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in blood vessel constriction, leading to decreased blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to improve blood vessel function and circulation. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to relax and dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving overall cardiovascular health. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing hypertension and improving overall heart health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with hypertension by improving blood vessel function and reducing inflammation, which in turn lowers blood pressure.
Bilobalide: This triterpene derivative helps with hypertension by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health.

Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpene lactones help with hypertension by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound that can increase blood pressure by promoting platelet aggregation and vasoconstriction.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help manage blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Seeds: They contain ginkgolides, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce blood pressure.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, similar to the leaves, which may help to regulate blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at high speed for 2 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powder with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 10 minutes or until the beeswax melts and the mixture becomes smooth.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hypertension by damaging blood vessels and increasing blood pressure.

Vasodilation-promoting compounds: Brahmi contains compounds like terpenoids and glycosides that help relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and lower blood pressure by causing vasodilation (widening of the blood vessels).

Adaptogenic and stress-reducing properties: Brahmi's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, reducing the negative impact of stress on blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri, also known as brahmi, creams helps with hypertension because it is rich in bioactive compounds that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to changing situations, thereby reducing blood pressure and heart rate. Additionally, Bacopa monnieri creams contain antioxidants that protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to high blood pressure. By promoting overall cardiovascular health, Bacopa monnieri creams may help alleviate hypertension symptoms and improve overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a type of triterpenoid saponin found in Bacopa monnieri. They help with hypertension by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, leading to improved cardiovascular function.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, are a class of compounds found in Bacopa monnieri. They help with hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which contributes to blood pressure regulation.

Bacopaside I: Bacopaside I is a bacoside compound that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It helps with hypertension by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and oxidative stress markers in the blood.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bacosides, which are bioactive compounds that help in reducing stress and anxiety, and in turn, may help in managing hypertension.
Barks: They contain alkaloids and other compounds that may contribute to their potential use in managing hypertension-related conditions.
Roots: They are a significant source of bacosides and other compounds that may aid in reducing stress and anxiety, potentially helping to manage hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers at dawn when they are at their highest potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature dehydrator at 30-40°c for 24-48 hours to preserve its bioactive compounds.
Combine the dried plant material with 100-200 grams of carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in a blender.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40-50°c for 30 minutes to 1 hour to enhance the extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle the bacopa monnieri cream in a clean environment to preserve its potency for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce blood pressure by preventing the constriction of blood vessels and improving vascular function.
Potassium: Turmeric cream contains potassium, an essential mineral that helps regulate blood pressure by counteracting the effects of sodium and promoting healthy vasodilation.

Vasodilatory compounds: The combination of curcumin and other bioactive compounds in turmeric cream helps to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving overall cardiovascular health by increasing peripheral resistance and decreasing cardiac workload.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with hypertension because of its active compound, curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin helps to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby lowering blood pressure. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to improve endothelial function, which is crucial for maintaining healthy blood pressure. By targeting the root causes of hypertension, Curcuma longa creams provide a natural and effective way to manage blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has been shown to help with hypertension by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key factors in the development of high blood pressure.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound present in turmeric, has been found to help with hypertension by reducing the production of angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor that contributes to high blood pressure.
Turmerone (α-Turmerone): Turmerone, a sesquiterpene compound, has been shown to have a vasodilatory effect, which can help lower blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving blood flow.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties that help lower blood pressure.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are used for their potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health by regulating blood pressure and preventing the formation of blood clots.

Seeds: Seeds of Curcuma longa contain compounds that may help to lower blood pressure by acting as vasodilators and preventing the constriction of blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and reducing hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried curcuma longa roots at the optimal time to ensure maximum potency.
Grind 50 grams of the harvested roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 50 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal marshmallow creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure by damaging blood vessels and increasing vascular resistance. This property helps to mitigate these effects and promote healthy blood vessel function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in herbal marshmallow creams, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and cardiovascular system, which is a major risk factor for hypertension. By decreasing inflammation, these compounds may help to lower blood pressure.

Potassium-rich properties: Marshmallow roots are a natural source of potassium, an essential mineral that helps regulate fluid balance and blood pressure. Consuming herbal marshmallow creams rich in potassium can help counterbalance the effects of sodium in the body and reduce blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with hypertension because of its unique properties. The root of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can also help to reduce stress, a common contributor to hypertension, making it a valuable natural remedy for those seeking to manage their blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Althaea oligosaccharides: They help with hypertension by reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health due to their ability to inhibit the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which plays a key role in blood pressure regulation.
Flavonoids: They aid in hypertension management by acting as vasodilators, causing blood vessels to relax and widen, thereby reducing blood pressure and improving blood flow.
Mucilages (polysaccharides): They help alleviate hypertension symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which are used to soothe and calm the blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to relax blood vessels and improve blood circulation, thus reducing hypertension.
Roots: Also contain other compounds like potassium and magnesium that help to balance electrolytes and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of althaea officinalis roots and dry them in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours at 50 degrees celsius.
Infuse 50 milliliters of water with the dried althaea roots for 10 minutes in a double boiler.
Strain the infusion using a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of petroleum jelly with 50 milliliters of the althaea infusion in a heat-resistant bowl.
Whip the mixture using a hand mixer until it thickens to a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with hypertension because they contain the herb Echinacea, which has been shown to help regulate blood pressure. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to its ability to reduce blood pressure by soothing and calming blood vessels, allowing them to relax and dilate. Additionally, Kansas coneflower creams have been found to increase the body's production of nitric oxide, a compound that helps to relax and widen blood vessels, further reducing blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory effects, which may help reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.

Saponins: These natural compounds have been found to lower blood pressure in animal studies by reducing peripheral resistance and improving endothelial function, potentially leading to better cardiovascular health.

Triterpenoids: Kan-sas coneflower contains a group of triterpenoids called echinocystic acids, which may help regulate blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help regulate blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
Barks: Contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots for the desired dosage of 30 grams per serving.
Combine the dried roots with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture at 60°c to 70°c for 1 hour and 15 minutes while stirring occasionally until the infusion is complete.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes of preparation time.
Allow the herbal cream to mature at room temperature for 24 hours before packaging in airtight containers with a shelf life of 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with hypertension because they contain flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds that work synergistically to improve cardiovascular health. The creams help to relax blood vessels, increasing blood flow and reducing peripheral resistance, which in turn lowers blood pressure. Additionally, the flavonoids have antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, further contributing to their blood-pressure-lowering effects. As a result, herbal hawthorn creams can be a valuable adjunct therapy for individuals managing hypertension.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn cream's flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, have been shown to improve blood vessel function by increasing nitric oxide production, which helps to relax and dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure.

Oligomeric Proanthocyanidins (OPCs): OPCs in hawthorn cream help to protect the lining of blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hypertension, while also improving blood flow and lowering blood pressure.

Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn's cardiac glycosides, such as digoxin, have a positive inotropic effect on the heart, meaning they help increase its pumping efficiency, reducing the workload on the heart and lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Barks: Contain bioactive compounds like quercetin, kaempferol, and epicatechin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for hypertension management.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in the morning, ensuring they are clean and free from any contaminants.
Dry the collected plant material using shade drying or air circulation for at least 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine one part dried crataegus monogyna with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil, infusing the mixture for 4-6 weeks under cold conditions.
Strain and filter the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding any solids and transferring the liquid to a clean glass container.

Mix one part of the filtered herbal extract with two parts emulsifier such as beeswax or cetyl alcohol, heating gently until smooth and creamy, then allowing it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with hypertension because they contain potent herbal extracts that help to relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce stress on the cardiovascular system. The creamy formula provides a soothing and calming effect, which can help to alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure such as anxiety and fatigue. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Chinese peony creams may also contribute to their blood pressure-lowering effects by reducing inflammation in the body's vascular tissues.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: The herbs in Chinese peony creams, such as Danshen (Salvia miltiorrhiza) and Gan Cao (Glycyrrhizae uraeensis), increase urine production to remove excess sodium and water from the body, helping to reduce blood pressure.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The antioxidants in Chinese peony creams, such as Baicalin (Scutellaria baicalensis) and Peony root extract, help to protect blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, which can contribute to hypertension.
Vasodilatory Properties: The flavonoids present in Chinese peony creams, such as Quercetin and Kaempferol, relax blood vessel walls, allowing for better blood flow and reducing blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which help to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: Contain starch and cellulose, which can help to reduce blood lipid levels and improve blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia lactiflora roots weighing 250 grams and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned paeonia lactiflora roots in a warm oven at 60°c for 3 hours then store them in air-tight containers.
Grind 100 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora root into fine powder using an electric grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered paeonia lactiflora with 200 milliliters of cold distilled water and let it steep for 2 hours at room temperature.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax, 25 grams of coconut oil, and the filtered paeonia lactiflora infusion in a heat-resistant glass bowl over low heat for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Selfheal creams helps with hypertension because they contain the herb Prunella vulgaris, which has been shown to have a significant impact on blood pressure levels. The cream's active compounds help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the strain on the cardiovascular system and lowering blood pressure. Additionally, selfheal's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress, which is often associated with hypertension. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal selfheal creams can provide a natural and effective approach to managing high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Selfheal cream's antioxidant compounds help protect the blood vessels from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Vasodilatory Effects: The cream's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to relax blood vessel walls, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory Activity: Selfheal cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to hypertension by causing vascular constriction and increasing peripheral resistance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the selfheal plant are used to make medicinal creams for hypertension due to their ability to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the plant, contain compounds that help relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating hypertension symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh prunella vulgaris leaves at dawn and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 6 hours or use a food dehydrator.
Grind 100g of dried prunella vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 200ml of distilled water and simmer for 30 minutes in a double boiler.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and combine it with 50g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil to create a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with hypertension because they contain compounds that have a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety levels. Lavender's natural sedative properties help to slow down heart rate and blood pressure, promoting relaxation and vasodilation. Additionally, lavender's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common underlying factors of hypertension. As a result, using English lavender creams can help to naturally lower blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.
remedy_properties: Lavender Oil's Calcium Channel Blocker Activity: Lavender oil has been shown to have a calcium channel blocker effect, which can help relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.

Antioxidant Properties: English lavender contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to hypertension. By reducing oxidative stress, lavender cream may help improve cardiovascular health and reduce blood pressure.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Effects: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system can help reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers of hypertension. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels, herbal English lavender creams may indirectly help lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Seeds: Containing sesquiterpenes and terpenoids that have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers, drying them for 2 weeks in a warm, well-ventilated area.
Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids and retaining liquid for use in cream production.
Whip together infused oil, beeswax, vitamin e oil, and shea butter in a double boiler until smooth and creamy, taking approximately 10-15 minutes to prepare.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with hypertension because they contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve circulation. This can help to lower blood pressure by reducing resistance in the arteries and allowing blood to flow more freely. Additionally, frankincense has been found to have a vasodilatory effect, causing blood vessels to dilate and further reducing blood pressure. By addressing the root causes of hypertension, frankincense creams may provide a natural and effective way to manage high blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce blood pressure by reducing inflammation in the blood vessels, which can constrict and increase blood pressure.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of frankincense cream can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to blood vessel walls, which can contribute to hypertension.
Vasodilatory: Frankincense cream's vasodilatory properties can help relax and widen blood vessels, reducing blood pressure by allowing blood to flow more freely through the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Frankincense roots have been traditionally used to help lower blood pressure and reduce stress, making them a key ingredient in creams for hypertension relief.
Barks: The barks of the frankincense tree contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest boswellia serrata gum resin from mature trees for optimal bioactive compound content.
Dry the harvested boswellia serrata gum resin in a cool dark place for 14 days to reduce moisture levels.
Grind the dried boswellia serrata gum resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for efficient mixing.
Mix 250mg of ground boswellia serrata with 10g of coconut oil and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat stir well and pour it into sterilized containers for storage and later application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai creams helps with hypertension because they contain compounds that have a positive impact on blood vessels and cardiovascular health. The cream's active ingredients, such as ferulic acid and lignans, help to relax constricted blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the heart. Additionally, dong quai has been shown to inhibit the production of angiotensin II, a hormone that constricts blood vessels and increases blood pressure. By addressing these underlying causes of hypertension, herbal dong quai creams can help to alleviate symptoms and promote overall cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_properties: Diosgenin: This saponin compound has been shown to relax blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure and improving blood flow, which can be beneficial for individuals with hypertension.

Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby contributing to a decrease in blood pressure.

Coumarin: This compound has anticoagulant properties, which can help to prevent the formation of blood clots and reduce blood viscosity, both of which are important factors in hypertension management.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the dong quai plant help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the dong quai plant contain flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects, helping to lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried angelica sinensis roots and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of ground angelica sinensis with 10 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 1 hour, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and dissolved.
Remove from heat and add 2 tablespoons of sweet almond oil and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil; mix well and let cool for 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hypertension are topical treatments that utilize plant extracts to help manage high blood pressure by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving cardiovascular health. The best herbal creams for hypertension include Rauvolfia serpentina, Centella asiatica, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Withania somnifera, Valeriana officinalis, Panax ginseng, Passiflora incarnata, Zingiber officinale, and Cassia auriculata. If left untreated, hypertension can lead to heart disease, stroke, and kidney damage, significantly impacting daily life, causing fatigue, shortness of breath, and increased risk of cardiac arrest.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hypertension that are treated with herbal creams are peripheral artery disease, varicose veins, and Raynaud's disease. Herbal creams containing ingredients like ginkgo biloba, butcher's broom, and horse chestnut can help alleviate symptoms such as poor circulation, swelling, and pain. These natural remedies work by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and strengthening vascular walls to promote overall cardiovascular health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with hypertension is often a blend of Ashwagandha, Lavender, and Ginkgo Biloba. Ashwagandha helps reduce stress and anxiety, while Lavender calms the mind and promotes relaxation. Ginkgo Biloba improves blood flow and lowers blood pressure by dilating blood vessels. A combination of these herbal creams can be applied topically to reduce inflammation and promote overall well-being. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any new creams, especially if you have pre-existing medical conditions or take medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina creams helps with hypertension because they contain an alkaloid called reserpine, which has been shown to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the reuptake of norepinephrine and serotonin. This process leads to a decrease in sympathetic nervous system activity, causing blood vessels to relax and dilate, resulting in lower blood pressure. Additionally, Rauvolfia serpentina has been found to reduce the heart rate and cardiac output, further contributing to its blood pressure-lowering effects, making it a popular natural remedy for managing hypertension.
remedy_constituents: Reserpine: It helps with hypertension by inhibiting the release of norepinephrine, a neurotransmitter that increases blood pressure.
Ajmaline: It helps with hypertension by blocking the alpha-adrenergic receptors, which are responsible for constricting blood vessels and increasing blood pressure.
Yohimbine: It helps with hypertension by blocking the alpha-2 adrenergic receptors, which are involved in vasoconstriction (narrowing of blood vessels) and increasing blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used to make creams for hypertension due to their high content of alkaloids, particularly reserpine, which helps to lower blood pressure.
Barks: Barks are also used to make creams for hypertension as they contain various alkaloids, including ajmaline and ajmalicine, which have vasodilatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used to make creams for hypertension due to their presence of alkaloids like reserpine and ajmaline, which contribute to their blood-pressure-lowering effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh rauvolfia serpentina roots in early morning to ensure highest alkaloid content.
Dry the harvested roots in shade for 7-10 days at 25-30°c to reduce moisture content.
Grind 20 grams of dried rauvolfia serpentina roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of powdered rauvolfia serpentina.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40-50°c for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams helps with hypertension because they contain triterpenoid saponins and other bioactive compounds that have vasodilatory properties, which help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow. This reduction in blood pressure is due to the increased nitric oxide production and the suppression of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE). As a result, Centella asiatica creams may help to lower blood pressure, reduce oxidative stress, and enhance overall cardiovascular health, providing natural relief from hypertension symptoms. Regular use may also improve overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help with hypertension by exerting a vasodilatory effect, relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow.
Asiaticoside: Contribute to lowering blood pressure by inhibiting the angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key component in the renin-angiotensin system that regulates blood pressure.
Madecassoside: Aid in reducing hypertension through its antioxidant properties, which help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most used part due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which are known for their antihypertensive and vasoprotective properties.
Roots: They are used due to their rich content of triterpenoid saponins and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health.

Stems: They are used because they contain bioactive compounds that have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antihypertensive effects, making them a valuable addition to creams for hypertension treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of centella asiatica leaves and flowers, wash them with cold water to remove dirt.
Infuse the centella asiatica in 200ml of carrier oil at 60°c for 2 hours to create a liquid extract.
Combine 10g of beeswax, 10g of shea butter, and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 20ml of the centella asiatica extract to the melted mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with hypertension because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that improve blood vessel function, allowing for better blood flow. This increased blood flow helps to lower blood pressure by reducing the resistance in blood vessels. The bioactive compounds in Ginkgo biloba also have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect blood vessels from damage and promote overall cardiovascular health. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may contribute to maintaining healthy blood pressure levels.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with hypertension by improving blood vessel function and reducing inflammation, which contributes to lower blood pressure.
Bilobalide: It helps with hypertension by acting as a vasodilator, causing blood vessels to relax and widen, which reduces blood pressure.
Ginkgolides: They help with hypertension by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, which contributes to reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part in Ginkgo biloba products due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have vasodilatory effects.
Seeds: The seeds contain a compound called ginkgotoxin, which has been linked to the reduction of blood pressure and may contribute to the antihypertensive effects of Ginkgo biloba creams.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, making them a potential ingredient in creams for hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Gently warm the carrier oil such as sweet almond oil 100ml by placing it in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba leaves 20g with the carrier oil in the double boiler and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Add the beeswax 20g to the mixture and heat the mixture gently until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify completely before applying to the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with hypertension because they contain 18-beta-glycyrrhetinic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 2 (11-beta-HSD2). This inhibition blocks the conversion of cortisone to cortisol, leading to a decrease in blood pressure. Additionally, glycyrrhizin in the creams has a vasodilatory effect, causing blood vessels to relax and widen, further contributing to reduced blood pressure. These mechanisms make glycyrrhiza glabra creams a potential adjunctive treatment for hypertension.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which plays a crucial role in blood pressure regulation.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids help with hypertension by exhibiting vasodilatory properties, increasing nitric oxide levels, and improving endothelial function, leading to reduced blood pressure.
Licoricidin: Licoricidin helps with hypertension by blocking the sodium-retaining effects of aldosterone, a hormone that can contribute to high blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to regulate blood pressure.
Barks: They are used to obtain glycosides, including glycyrrhizin, which can help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Steep 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in 100ml of distilled water for 30 minutes to create an infusion.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Mix 10 grams of shea butter with 20 grams of jojoba oil in a separate bowl until well combined.
Combine the infused water with the melted wax mixture and mix until a smooth emulsion forms.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of essential oil to the mixture and whip until thickened.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera creams helps with hypertension because they contain withanolides, a group of bioactive compounds that have been shown to have adaptogenic properties, helping the body to cope with stress and promote balance in the cardiovascular system. The creams may also lower cortisol levels, which can contribute to high blood pressure. Additionally, withanolides have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to relax blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health, ultimately contributing to reduced blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Withania somnifera alkaloids help with hypertension by exerting a protective effect on the cardiovascular system, reducing blood pressure and preventing cardiac fibrosis.

Withanolides: Withanolides, a group of steroidal lactones, have been shown to help with hypertension by improving blood vessel function, reducing vascular resistance, and exerting antioxidant effects.

Saponins: Saponins in Withania somnifera help with hypertension by reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving lipid profiles, and exerting a diuretic effect that helps lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of withanolides, which have been shown to have antihypertensive properties and can help regulate blood pressure.
Leaves: They are rich in withanolides and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypertension.
Seeds: They contain withanolides and other phytochemicals that may help regulate blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried withania somnifera roots and clean them thoroughly.
Steep 250 grams of the roots in 2 liters of carrier oil at 60-70 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then filter it through a cheesecloth.
Combine 100 grams of the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture to 50-60 degrees celsius and whip it until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with hypertension because they contain the active compounds found in Valerian root, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The sedative properties of Valerian root help to calm the nervous system, leading to a decrease in blood pressure. The creams also increase the production of nitric oxide, a natural vasodilator that widens blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more freely and reducing pressure. This natural approach can help to regulate blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps with hypertension by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can lead to a reduction in sympathetic nervous system activity and a subsequent decrease in blood pressure.
Valerenol: It helps with hypertension by inhibiting the activity of voltage-gated calcium channels, which can reduce the contraction of blood vessels and lower blood pressure.
Valeranone: It helps with hypertension by blocking the action of the sympathetic nervous system, particularly the release of noradrenaline, which can contribute to increased blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of valerenic acid, a key compound that helps in reducing blood pressure and promoting relaxation.
Leaves: Valerian leaves contain valerenol and valeranone, which contribute to the anti-hypertensive properties of valerian creams.
Buds: Valerian buds are rich in valerenic acid and other compounds that help in calming the nervous system and lowering blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and mix it with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes and then pour it into a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams helps with hypertension because they contain ginsenosides, bioactive compounds that have been shown to have a positive effect on blood pressure regulation. The creams help to relax blood vessels, improve blood circulation, and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system. This results in a decrease in blood pressure and a reduction in the risk of cardiovascular disease. Additionally, ginseng has been found to have antioxidant properties, which help to protect against oxidative stress and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds help lower blood pressure by improving cardiovascular function, reducing inflammation, and relaxing blood vessels.
Saponins: Saponins help reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which constricts blood vessels and increases blood pressure.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids help reduce blood pressure by reducing inflammation, improving endothelial function, and inhibiting the activity of various enzymes involved in blood pressure regulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ginsenosides, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Leaves: Although less studied than roots and rhizomes, they still contain some ginsenosides and may contribute to the overall antihypertensive properties of the extracts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried panax ginseng roots from a trusted supplier.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 1 gram of the ground ginseng powder with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20-30 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Whip the mixture into a creamy consistency using a hand mixer for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with hypertension because they contain Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, which has been traditionally used to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The cream's ability to calm the nervous system can contribute to lower blood pressure levels. Studies have shown that the flavonoids and alkaloids present in Passiflora incarnata have a positive effect on cardiovascular health, making it a potential natural remedy for managing hypertension. Regular use of Passiflora incarnata creams may help alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with hypertension by reducing oxidative stress and improving blood vessel function, which leads to a decrease in blood pressure.

Isomers of flavonoids (e.g., vitexin and isovitexin): These compounds have a direct effect on the cardiovascular system by causing vasodilation and reducing the heart rate, ultimately lowering blood pressure.

Alkaloids (e.g., harman and harmine): These compounds help with hypertension by modulating the autonomic nervous system and reducing the release of stress hormones, which in turn decreases blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracted leaves are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to have a calming effect and help lower blood pressure.
Buds: Passiflora incarnata buds contain a high amount of flavonoids and glycosides, which can help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
Stems: The stems of Passiflora incarnata are used because they contain a rich combination of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can help to lower blood pressure and reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom for 2 weeks.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 40c for 24 hours.
Steep 20g of dried passiflora incarnata in 100ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material.
Mix 10g of the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 10ml of distilled water in a double boiler for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to improve circulation and reduce blood pressure naturally. The active compound, gingerol, present in Zingiber officinale, has potent vasodilatory properties that help to relax blood vessels and increase blood flow. This, in turn, reduces the strain on the heart and lowers blood pressure. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to its blood pressure-lowering effects and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help with hypertension by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to blood vessel constriction and increased blood pressure.
Shogaols: Help with hypertension by reducing the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key enzyme involved in the regulation of blood pressure.
Terpenes (specifically, geranial and neral): Help with hypertension by exerting vasodilatory effects, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and blood pressure-lowering properties.

Roots: Roots are another commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their rich content of gingerols and shogaols, which are believed to help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.

Stems: Stems are occasionally used in creams for hypertension due to their lesser-known but potentially beneficial compounds that may help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate cardiovascular symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract zingiber officinale rhizomes from the root system using a sharp knife or tool within 5 minutes.
Grind the extracted rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 1/4 cup of distilled water to create a paste within 2 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax to melt the mixture at 180 degrees for 5 minutes.
Blend the melted mixture with the zingiber officinale paste and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream within 3 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata creams helps with hypertension because they contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, which help in relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow. Additionally, Cassia auriculata has been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypertension. The cream's topical application allows for direct delivery of these active compounds to the skin, promoting a potential reduction in blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These flavonoids help with hypertension by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin helps with hypertension by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key regulator of blood pressure, and by improving vascular function.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and kaempferol-3-O-beta-D-glucopyranoside): These compounds help with hypertension by blocking the action of ACE and by scavenging free radicals, leading to a reduction in blood pressure and improvement in cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cassia auriculata leaves are used due to their anthraquinone content, which helps to lower blood pressure.
Roots: Cassia auriculata roots are used due to their flavonoid content, which helps to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Seeds: Cassia auriculata seeds are used due to their alkaloid content, which helps to lower blood pressure and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cassia auriculata flowers and leaves in the early morning when they are still moist with dew.
Dry the harvested cassia auriculata flowers and leaves in a shaded area for 7-10 days.
Extract the herbal properties from dried cassia auriculata flowers and leaves using 500ml of carrier oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Mix the extracted herbal oil with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in a cool dark place for 24 hours before use.
